char.name: Reuben
char.bio: Firstborn of Jacob, eager, but unsteady as water. Found mandrakes in the field for my mother.

friends.name: Leah
friends.bio: Eldest daughter of Laban, the one Jacob did not choose. But the LORD has seen me, and He fills my arms with sons. 💧

friends.name: Jacob
friends.bio: Younger twin with a strong grip, they call me the heel-grabber. Saw a ladder to heaven once. Still chasing the blessing. 🪜

friends.name: Benjamin
friends.bio: Youngest of the twelve. My mother's last breath named me son of sorrow, but my father calls me son of his right hand.

friends.name: Joseph
friends.bio: Son of Rachel, the dreamer of the family. Had the strangest dream last night about sheaves and stars... ✨

friends.name: Simeon
friends.bio: Second son of Leah. Quick to draw the sword when my sister's honor is on the line.

friends.name: Judah
friends.bio: Fourth son of Leah, and this time, I will praise the LORD. Learning my way, for better and for worse. 🦁

friends.name: Lot
friends.bio: Son of Haran, traveling with my uncle Abram. Looking for well-watered land for my flocks.

friends.name: Abram
friends.bio: Called to leave my country and my father's house. God promised to make me a great nation.

friends.name: Sarai
friends.bio: Wife of Abram. Following my husband into the unknown. Barren, but keeping faith.

friends.name: Rebekah
friends.bio: Drew water for a stranger and all his camels, and it changed my whole life. Veiled and on the road to meet my husband. ✨

friends.name: Esau
friends.bio: Firstborn of Isaac. A man of the open field, give me the hunt and the red hills. That stew? Worth it at the time. 🏹

friends.name: Rachel
friends.bio: Shepherdess of Haran. Jacob worked seven years for me, and to him they seemed but a few days for the love he had. 💍

posts.author: CHAR_176
posts.body: I am the one who tried to save him from the pit and failed. I have carried that failure for twenty-two years, and every time our father wept for Joseph a little more of me died. And today the governor of all Egypt turned out to be the brother I could not save, alive, powerful, and instead of destroying us he wept on our necks and fed us and told us not to hate ourselves. I do not know what to do with grace this size. We came down here as guilty men expecting judgment, and we are going home forgiven, with wagons full of grain and the impossible news that will bring our father back from the grave he has been living in. Joseph is alive. After everything we did. Joseph is alive.
posts.highlight: the brothers, expecting judgment, receive forgiveness (Gen 45:9-15)

author_info.name: Reuben
author_info.bio: Firstborn of Jacob, eager, but unsteady as water. Found mandrakes in the field for my mother.

posts.author: CHAR_176
posts.body: It went too far out at Dothan and I could not stop it. The others saw Joseph coming in that coat and something in them turned murderous, here comes the dreamer, let us kill him and throw him in a pit and see what becomes of his dreams. I am the eldest. I talked them down to just throwing him in the pit, no blood on our hands, and I meant to come back and pull him out and take him home to our father when the rest had gone. I stepped away for one hour. One hour. When I came back the pit was empty. They had sold him. I tore my clothes. I am the firstborn and I could not save my own brother, and now I have to go help them lie to our father. God have mercy on this family. God have mercy on me.
posts.highlight: the brothers plot murder; Reuben intends to rescue Joseph from the pit but is too late (Gen 37:18-30)

author_info.name: Reuben
author_info.bio: Firstborn of Jacob, eager, but unsteady as water. Found mandrakes in the field for my mother.

posts.author: CHAR_176
posts.body: I will not hide it. My father knows and God knows, and hiding it now would only be one more sin stacked on the pile. While we were camped near Migdal Eder, I slept with Bilhah, my father's concubine. My father's woman. I told myself it was about the old wound, my mother Leah shoved aside for Rachel even after Rachel was in the ground, and Bilhah taking a place I felt belonged to us. I told myself a great many things that night. Not one of them is true enough to matter. Israel heard of it. He did not shout. He never shouts anymore. He just went quiet, and looked at me differently from that day on, and I understood that I had lost something I could never buy back. I am the firstborn of Israel. And I threw it away in one hot, stupid hour, the same way my uncle Esau threw his away for a bowl of stew. We never learn in this family. We never learn.
posts.highlight: Reuben sleeps with Bilhah, his father's concubine; Israel hears of it (Gen 35:22)
posts.ember: Reuben forfeits the dignity of the firstborn by this sin (Gen 49:3-4); the birthright folly of Esau repeated in the next generation

author_info.name: Reuben
author_info.bio: Firstborn of Jacob, eager, but unsteady as water. Found mandrakes in the field for my mother.
